Intraday post (02/March)

Weekly post:

West-East war is escalating, Hormuz, Oil, Inflation | Weekly post (2-6/March)

Summary first

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

This seemingly contradicts the OD heatmap, but the net speed profile is negative (see my chart). But way more vol is being sold to the downside, market aims to defend the downside below the pivot. Volatility is priced high. Upside speed is not that steep, market’s primal goal is to manage volatility.

Centroid is at 6747.90 This is a locally supportive range in terms of vol, market expects any downside momentum to slowdown here.

Downside pivot is at 6684.66target is 6680.73Locally, speed is steep. This is a sticky band, expect magnetic effect below 6710. This is were market expects the most support.

Upside pivot is at 6863.19target 6888.47 The pivot is sticky. Market expect momentum to be breaken and pin to the pivot here.

However I would mark 6820/30 band as a line-in-the-sand and a serious reversion line towards any direction. Market expects volatile reaction from this line, like a center of a long iron fly trade (but it isn’t that technically). To the downside there are lot of put spreads sold to brake the momentum: 6800/6790, 6785/6745 etc. Upside stabilization is only possible above 6900, but market doesn’t bet on that. Primal goal is to defend the downside.

Below 6962.48 for ES, and 6943.91 for SPX, the downside pressure remains.

Below 6962.48 for ES, and 6943.91 for SPX, the downside pressure remains.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
